title: Offenses During Employment and Corresponding Penalties
content: This section enumerates various offenses committed by seafarers during employment and prescribes penalties for first, second, and third offenses. Offenses include smuggling or violation of customs rules (including smuggling taxable items, possession/use of prohibited drugs, gun-running, abetting smuggling, misdeclaration of articles, possession of pornographic or child pornography materials), desertion, abandoning post, leaving ship without permission, unauthorized delegation of duties, sleeping on post, insubordination (disobedience, assault, disrespect, insulting, inciting insubordination), drunkenness (on duty, causing trouble, failure to perform duties), and creating trouble outside ship premises implicating the ship. Penalties escalate from suspension from participation in the overseas employment program (ranging from six months to three years depending on offense and repetition) to permanent disqualification from participation in the overseas employment program upon third offense or in some cases first offense (e.g., desertion).
